LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT STARBUCKS CORPORATION'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS GRANTED. STARBUCKS PRESENTED SUFFICIENT EVIDENCE TO SATISFY ITS SUMMARY MOTION BURDEN THAT IT IS ENTITLED TO JUDGMENT IN ITS FAVOR. MS. NOVARESI DID NOT FILE ANY OPPOSITION TO THIS MOTION. PLAINTIFF'S EX PARTE APPLICATION TO CONTINUE THE HEARING IS DENIED. ORDER IS SIGNED. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN, CLERK: S. MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION .
Off Calendar Mar-11-2022 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE:
Julia Young, Esq. (916.296.0786; juliayoung21@hotmail.com) for Plaintiff.
Emily Genge, Esq. (510.463.8600; egenge@grsm.com) of Gordon Rees Scully Mansukhani, LLP for Defendant.

Hearing is held (as fully reported in the reporter's notes). The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

DEFENDANT STARBUCKS CORPORATION'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION is granted. Starbucks presented sufficient evidence to satisfy its summary motion burden that it is entitled to judgment in its favor. Ms. Novaresi did not file any opposition to this motion.

Plaintiff's ex parte application to continue the hearing is denied.

Order is signed.

Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: S. Le, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/HEK)
